Common Problems

Signs Your Garage Door Opener Needs Repair

Most opener problems have a clear cause. Our technicians diagnose the exact issue on the first visit — no guesswork, no unnecessary parts.

Opener Won't Respond

Remote and wall button do nothing. Usually a faulty logic board, blown capacitor, or power issue — all repairable same-day.

Motor Runs, Door Doesn't Move

The motor hums or runs but the door stays still. Almost always a stripped drive gear — one of the most common opener repairs we see.

Opener Makes Grinding Noise

Loud grinding means the drive gear or sprocket is wearing out. Catching this early saves you from a complete opener replacement.

Remote Stops Working

Remote worked fine then stopped. Could be a dead battery, a broken remote, or a logic board that lost programming — we test all three.

Door Reverses Immediately

Door starts to close then reverses right away. Usually misaligned or dirty safety sensors — a quick fix that our techs handle in minutes.

Opener Runs Slowly or Stops Mid-Cycle

A weakening motor, incorrect force settings, or a failing capacitor can cause sluggish or incomplete cycles. We diagnose and fix the root cause.

Our Process

How We Diagnose & Fix Your Opener

We follow a systematic diagnostic process to find the exact problem fast — so we fix it right the first time.

1

Full System Diagnosis

We test the motor, logic board, drive gear, sensors, remote signal, and power supply to pinpoint the exact cause of the failure.

2

Upfront Quote

We explain the problem clearly and give you a transparent quote before touching anything. No surprises — you approve before we proceed.

3

Repair with Quality Parts

We carry common replacement parts — gears, logic boards, capacitors, sensors — in our vans for same-day repairs without waiting for orders.

4

Full Test & Reprogram

After repair we run full open/close cycles, reprogram remotes if needed, test safety reverse, and verify everything works perfectly before leaving.
